name: Las Vegas
tagline: No State Income Tax, Endless Entertainment, and Desert Sun
description: Las Vegas offers FIRE retirees a unique combination of zero state income tax, surprisingly affordable housing outside the Strip corridor, and world-class entertainment at every price point. The desert climate means low humidity and over 300 sunny days per year. While the city's reputation centers on gambling, locals enjoy a growing food scene, Red Rock Canyon hiking, and easy access to Utah's national parks.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.climate: Desert with extremely hot summers and mild winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Henderson or Summerlin South

dining.description: Cheap casino buffets and fast-casual spots 2-3x/week; mostly cooking at home

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan; Nevada exchange options are moderate

transportation.description: Older paid-off car with insurance and gas; a car is essential here

entertainment.description: Free Strip walks, Red Rock Canyon hikes, occasional discounted show tickets

1M.lifestyle: You live in a suburban area like Henderson or Summerlin South, well away from the Strip, and cook most meals at home. Cheap casino buffet deals and casual restaurants help stretch the dining budget a couple of times a week. Summer electricity bills for air conditioning are a real cost in the desert heat. No state income tax helps, and free entertainment on the Strip is always available if you avoid the gambling floor.

housing.description: Nice 2BR apartment in Summerlin or a condo in Green Valley

dining.description: Mid-range restaurants 3-4x/week; occasional Strip dining deals

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision coverage

transportation.description: Reliable mid-range car with full coverage insurance

entertainment.description: Regular show tickets, gym membership, golf at public courses, day trips to Valley of Fire

2M.lifestyle: You can rent a nice apartment in Summerlin or Green Valley and eat out several times a week at mid-range restaurants, with occasional Strip dining when deals are available. Show tickets, a gym membership, and golf at public courses fit the budget. Weekend road trips to Zion, the Grand Canyon, or Valley of Fire are easy drives. No state income tax means your full withdrawal is yours to spend.

housing.description: 3BR house in Summerlin or Southern Highlands with a pool

dining.description: Fine dining on the Strip at restaurants by celebrity chefs; regular sushi and steakhouse visits

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with comprehensive coverage

transportation.description: Late-model SUV, premium insurance, occasional rideshare to the Strip

entertainment.description: Premium show seats, golf club membership, spa visits, regular concerts and events

3M.lifestyle: You can rent a house with a pool in Summerlin or Southern Highlands and dine regularly at the celebrity-chef restaurants on the Strip. Premium show tickets, a golf club membership, and regular spa visits fit comfortably. Good private health insurance and enough room for Southwest road trips round out a comfortable life. Nevada's zero income tax is a meaningful advantage at this spending level.

housing.description: Luxury home in The Ridges, MacDonald Highlands, or a high-rise condo on the Strip

dining.description: VIP dining experiences at Joël Robuchon, Bazaar Meat; private chef for home entertaining

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine with executive health screenings

transportation.description: Luxury vehicle, premium insurance, car detailing service

entertainment.description: VIP nightlife, premium golf club, courtside NBA seats, exclusive shows and events

domesticHelp.description: Bi-weekly deep cleaning, pool service, landscaping, occasional personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: High-roller experiences, quarterly luxury travel, spa packages, fine wine collection

5M.lifestyle: A large home in a gated community like The Ridges or MacDonald Highlands, regular dining at the best Strip restaurants, and a housekeeper who comes several times a week. Concierge medicine, a nice car, and business class flights are standard. Premium golf club membership, courtside NBA seats, and the best show tickets in town fit comfortably. Nevada's zero income tax keeps the full withdrawal intact.

housing.description: Custom estate in The Summit or penthouse suite in a luxury Strip tower

dining.description: Personal chef, exclusive private dining rooms, catered events and parties

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge medical practice, executive wellness programs, holistic care

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private car service, occasional charter flights

entertainment.description: Private casino hosts, front-row everything, art collecting, international travel, philanthropy

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, personal assistant, pool and grounds maintenance team

luxuryExtras.description: Private jet trips, luxury suite entertaining, real estate investments, charitable foundations

10M.lifestyle: Premium housing in The Summit or a penthouse in a luxury Strip tower, regular fine dining, a cook who comes several times a week, and a housekeeper. First class travel, full concierge healthcare, and front-row access to any show or event in the city. Nevada's zero income tax at this spending level is a significant financial advantage. Las Vegas's entertainment infrastructure means there is always something to do without needing to travel far.
